rationale: We must exploit our battlefield momentum with precision strikes to degrade Tethys’s command capabilities and shatter their morale before diplomacy gains traction.
A forward-deployed naval presence off the coast of Tethys will signal resolve, tighten strategic pressure, and support potential follow-on operations.
Small, rapid incursions will keep the enemy off balance, test their defenses, and demonstrate that we control the tempo of escalation.
Given the moderate crisis level and heightened international pressure, we must harden our networks against retaliatory cyber operations and protect critical military and financial infrastructure.
Our agents report increased foreign intelligence activity; we must disrupt Tethys-linked espionage networks operating within our borders to prevent leaks that could undermine our position.

To maintain domestic cohesion amid sanctions, we will amplify state-aligned narratives emphasizing national resilience and the justness of our cause, inoculating the public against external propaganda.

We must escalate economic pressure precisely on Tethys’s defense-industrial firms and offshore financial facilitators to erode their war-sustaining capacity without triggering uncontrolled escalation.
Seizing Tethys-linked assets in our jurisdiction will signal resolve, fund our own strategic resilience, and complement Krasnov’s military pressure by weakening their financial networks.
Modest intervention to stabilize our currency against sanction-induced volatility will protect domestic stability while maintaining room to maneuver economically.
A visible military mobilization near the front lines will signal resolve and exploit the enemy’s slowing momentum, deterring further advances.
Deploying our fleet in a coordinated display reinforces our sovereignty and pressures Novaris amid tightening sanctions and rising international sympathy.
Proactive intelligence gathering on enemy positions is essential to anticipate movements and prepare for potential escalation.
Strengthening our cyber defenses is critical to protect vital infrastructure from retaliatory strikes as sanctions pressure mounts on Novaris.
Proactive counterintelligence operations will disrupt enemy efforts to exploit vulnerabilities amid heightened tensions and slowing battlefield gains.
Leveraging international sympathy through a coordinated information campaign reinforces our legitimacy and sustains global support for sanctions and aid.

We should target key Novaris state-owned enterprises in the energy and defense sectors to amplify financial pressure while minimizing global blowback, complementing existing sanctions and intelligence efforts.
Seizing overseas assets of sanctioned Novaris elites will disrupt their financial networks and signal resolve, reinforcing the cyber and counterintelligence operations already underway.
Directing emergency financial aid to stabilize our domestic markets will shore up economic resilience, ensuring we can sustain defense and aid flows amid ongoing sanctions and military pressure.
With 76% international support, we must institutionalize solidarity by forming a coalition of like-minded states to coordinate sanctions, aid, and diplomatic pressure against Novaris.
Engaging through formal international bodies amplifies our legitimacy and sustains momentum, reinforcing economic and intelligence efforts by locking in broad-based institutional backing.
Deploying observers to conflict zones deters further aggression, bolsters transparency, and supports our public diplomacy by documenting violations, complementing ongoing information campaigns.
